hook: Small repairs matter more than many men realize in early relationships.
body: The ability to acknowledge a miss, clarify intent, and reconnect calmly is a major trust builder.

hook: Pride can make men defend tiny errors as if admitting them would weaken their position.
body: Owning a miss quickly often strengthens your credibility rather than reducing it.

hook: Owning a miss quickly often strengthens your credibility rather than reducing it.
body: If your tone landed poorly, a simple apology and clearer restatement usually works better than arguing about whether she should have taken it that way.

body: Practice fast repair: name the miss, own your part, and reset the tone without dragging it out.
